dialogos.texto_en: Have we all finished exploring the Shurijo Castle?
Yeah, that was fun!\nNot only just the buildings, but the view was spectacular.
Was it Iri-no Azana?\nThe view from there was amazing,\nyou could see the whole city.
Yeah, yeah!\nThat place was wonderful!
Apparently, you can also watch the sunset disappear into the sea.\nThat's definitely gotta be romantic,\nso I want to come back in the evening to see it.
For me, it was definitely the main temple.\nI was able to reflect on the history\nof the Ryukyu Kingdom.
I'm with Itsuki-san! The picture of the dragon\ncarved on the bright red pillar was beautiful.
What about you, Miku-san?\nWhat was your favorite part of Shurijo Castle?
For me, it was the stone walls.\nI realized how magnificent of a castle Shurijo is.
How old-fashioned.\nBut that's something you'd say, Miku.
For me, it was the relaxing atmosphere of the 'okushoin.'\nIsn't that right, Miku?
Y-yeah, you're right.
Oh?\nWhen did you two have a little date?
A D-DATE?!
Hey, don't jump to conclusions like that.
Okay, next up! We've got the Prefectural Museum,\nnicknamed 'Okimyu.'
Okimyu?\nIs that a word in Okinawan? What does it mean?
It's short for Okinawa Museum.
Ah I get it.
Is that where you want to go, Miku-san?\nDo you like museums?
Uh, well, not exactly.
You'd said earlier\nyou were interested in the special exhibition, Miku.
Yeah.\nIt features the relationship\nbetween the warlords of Kyushu and the Ryukyu Kingdom.
Oh, I see.\nThat's something that you can't miss, Miku.
Yeah, I'm really excited.
Okay, let's go.
This time for sure, I'll go together with Futaro.
Miku? Hurry up, hurry up!
O-okay! I'm coming!
